hero.heading: Shape the conversation at Devcon
hero.body: Programming at Devcon India will engage 10,000+ builders, researchers, designers, and thinkers from across the decentralized ecosystem.
hero.body_strong: Got something you want to share? Submit your application.

cards.title: 10,000+ attendees
cards.p1: Devcon is where the people behind decentralized systems come together to learn, build, and connect.
cards.p2: Speaking at Devcon allows you to reach those communities directly.

cards.title: Open to everyone
cards.p1: There's no speaker bureau or invite list and speaker applications are fully open to the general public.
cards.p2_prefix: If you have knowledge to share on our
cards.p2_suffix: , we want to hear from you.

cards.title: Put principles into practice
cards.p1: Devcon is where open source, privacy, security, and censorship resistance move from ideas into real systems.
cards.p2: If your work makes these principles more accessible, share it on the Devcon stage.

formats.heading: Select a session format
formats.intro: There’s multiple ways to speak and present at Devcon India. Choose the format that fits your content best.

cards.title: Lightning Talk
cards.description: Present a sharp, focused idea. Ideal if you're new to conference speaking.

cards.title: Talk
cards.description: A longer form, in-depth presentation on a topic of your choice.

cards.title: Mixed Format
cards.description: A conversational session – AMA, fireside chat, or roundtable discussion.

cards.title: Workshop
cards.description: A hands-on, skills-focused session with active audience participation.

formats.free_ticket_note: As a thank you for contributing to Devcon India, all selected speakers will receive a free ticket.

tracks.heading: Find your Track
tracks.intro_line_1: Devcon India covers nine tracks across the decentralized and Ethereum ecosystems.
tracks.intro_line_2: Apply to any track where your work or thinking fits.

items.title: Core Protocol
items.description: Researchers and developers have worked for over ten years on building and improving Ethereum. What are the latest findings, what's on the roadmap, and what does Ethereum need to become to still be Ethereum in twenty years?

items.title: Privacy & Consent
items.description: Talks about privacy, digital identity, and what consent means as identity systems become more prevalent. Grounded in research and technical merit, from self-sovereign identity to the communities shaped by these systems.

items.title: Security
items.description: Threat modeling, operational security, and what it takes to protect people who cannot afford to be wrong.

items.title: Futures Worth Building
items.description: The forward-looking conversations, the societal challenges of today, and the futures still open to us. How communities, public goods teams, artists, and builders can reclaim agency over the choices that shape them.

items.title: Rights, Freedoms & Governance
items.description: Honest case studies of where digital tools have delivered on rights and self-governance, and where they have failed. Deployed in the contexts that matter most.

items.title: Permissionless Networks
items.description: Open networks, censorship resistance, and the infrastructure that keeps connectivity in the hands of its users. What survives when the network layer is hostile?

items.title: Users, Builders & Agents
items.description: The people building on Ethereum, the people using it, and the agents now working alongside both. What does it take to make that experience secure, private and usable?

items.title: Applied Cryptography
items.description: Cryptographic primitives like ZKP, MPC and FHE are advancing rapidly. What can we build now that we couldn't last year, and how do we put these breakthroughs to work?

items.title: Open & Verifiable Stack
items.description: From silicon to application, what does it take to trust the full stack? Open hardware, firmware, and the software that runs on it.

process.heading: The process

cards.line_1: Have an idea for a session?

process.callout: Reviewers’ decisions are final. There is no appeal process.

final_cta.heading: Apply to Speak at Devcon
final_cta.body_prefix: Over 3,000 people applied to speak at Devcon SEA – so please wait for our response. We’re aiming to start review decisions
final_cta.contact_prefix: Got a question? Reach out to
